concerns.text: Tasks T030, T039, T040, and T041 implement token-level confidence extraction and Expected Calibration Error (ECE) analysis. These deliverables are not present in spec.md (FR-001 to FR-013) or plan.md. The 'Critical Revision' note claims these address a review concern, but the spec/plan were not updated to authorize this scope. This is unapproved scope creep violating the Single Source of Truth (Constitution Principle IV) and FR-030.

Task T020 (logging excluded perturbations) is marked incomplete `[ ]`. However, spec.md FR-002 and FR-003 explicitly require: 'Invalid or semantically divergent perturbations MUST be logged... while storing raw scores for all candidates.' Without T020, the deliverable falls short of the FR, violating the requirement that the task satisfy the spec it claims to cover.

Task T029 implements a budget cap logic. While FR-011 requires a budget cap, the spec mandates generating 'up to 3 perturbation variants' (FR-002). The plan.md 'Complexity Tracking' section unilaterally narrows this to 'A single perturbation per task' without a formal spec amendment. If T029 implements the plan's 'single perturbation' logic, it silently weakens the spec's 'up to 3' requirement. If it implements 'up to 3', it contradicts the plan. The task description is ambiguous and risks violating the spec's volume requirement.

Task T037 implements sensitivity analysis on 'a specific set of values'. Spec.md FR-009 and FR-013 explicitly mandate the set {0.85, 0.90, 0.95, 0.99}. The task description uses vague phrasing ('specific set') which risks implementing a different set, thereby failing to satisfy the explicit acceptance criteria of FR-009/FR-013.
